label: PUBLIC_SKILL_AUTHORING_API_CONTRACT_DRIFT
output_md:
# skill_boundary_brief.md
Create `api_contract_drift_check` as a reusable Soulforge skill for reviewing public-safe API contract drift packets. Keep runtime inputs outside canon. The skill must separate confirmed evidence from assumptions and keep private data, customer endpoints, production logs, credentials, incident detail, and `_workspaces` files out of tracked skill content.

# skill_package_draft.md
Package scope: `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/skill.yaml`, `README.md`, optional `codex/SKILL.md`, and only public-safe templates or checklists. The skill should guide an agent through drift review, owner mapping, blocker identification, evidence-first remediation ordering, and a bounded handoff note. It should work on redacted packets and avoid project-specific endpoint names.

# skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Recommended bundle: a short packet schema checklist, an evidence-vs-assumption worksheet, a remediation ordering template, and a handoff note template. Any helper script must accept synthetic/redacted packet files only and must not encode live route names or repository-specific details. No private fixtures, secrets, or production traces belong in the bundle.

# skill_boundary_review.md
Boundary checks: confirmed drift may reference changed endpoints, type deltas, route-handler touchpoints, and failing tests only when present in the packet. Unconfirmed claims must stay labeled as assumptions. The skill must warn when packet content is incomplete, when ownership is unclear, or when evidence does not support remediation priority.

# skill_install_sync_request.md
Prepare an install handoff note that tells the operator how to sync the tracked package into the local installed mirror. The note should state that the skill package was authored for handoff and that sync itself is an operating step, not something the skill claims to have completed.

# skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke with a tiny synthetic packet containing two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route handler note, and one failing test name. Pass criteria: the skill returns confirmed evidence, explicit assumptions, owner handoff guidance, remediation order, and boundary warnings without exposing forbidden runtime data.

# skill_release_review.md
Release readiness is conditional on the package staying public-safe, reusable, and bounded to redacted inputs. The skill is suitable for promotion if it preserves evidence separation, avoids project-specific canon, and keeps install sync as a handoff request rather than a claimed action.
